final_answer:
Yes, for Phase 4 scope this is complete; I did not find a functional implementation gap.

Why this matters: the current state now enforces the three Phase 4 objectives around `ValidatorJoin` authorization, no bridge-driven validator-join execution path, and ranked/capped validator-set behavior, which keeps Gold Chain aligned with the final native validator architecture.

Confirmed (read-only):
1. Join sender/signer auth test exists and checks rejection + no validator creation in [msg_server_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/keeper/msg_server_test.go:62).
2. No bridge-join listener/processor runtime path exists in non-test code (`sendValidatorJoinToGiltConsensus`, `handleStakedLog`, `case helper.StakedEvent` not found), and guard tests are present in [rootchain_log_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/listener/rootchain_log_test.go:123) and [stake_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake_test.go:91).
3. Ranked validator-set behavior coverage is present, including cap, boundary swap, tie-break, and non-current exclusion in [validator_set_selection_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/x/stake/types/validator_set_selection_test.go:21).

Checks run (all pass):
- `go test ./x/stake/keeper ./bridge/listener ./bridge/processor ./x/stake/types -count=1`

What should be done next:
1. Proceed to the next phase.
2. Optional hardening: add one runtime-style bridge task registration/dispatch test (not source-text-based) for stronger future refactor safety.
prompt: Read only task, don't change code. Go through fine tooth comb of what you just implemented and check for gaps or partial implementation and report back please. Dont be overzealous and go on tangents, remain in the confines of the scope of this task, there might be times where you might have to say all done nothing left, which would also be an acceptable response
